pattern_E_aggregate_adjacency_vs_per_pap.description: Verifier scores per-paper coverage (no single paper covers the multi-feature composition >= 0.7); primary scores broad-adjacency cluster coverage (multiple aspects collectively reach >= 0.7). Both apply program_v5.md §2 functional-equivalence rubric in good faith. Difference is interpretation of 'same functional effect' across multi-feature compositions where each feature individually has prior art but the combination doesn't.
pattern_E_aggregate_adjacency_vs_per_pap.intensification_cause: Increasing mechanism-component count per candidate (4-5 distinct sub-mechanisms standard in E21+) plus increasing prior-art retrieved (mean 8 hits per round on primary). Each component matches separately; combination doesn't match any single paper.
pattern_E_aggregate_adjacency_vs_per_pap.recommend_v6_action: Document Pattern E with calibration guidance; consider explicit per-paper vs aggregate-adjacency scoring channels OR tighten aggregate rubric to require all-components-must-have-prior-art-per-paper.
